Title: The glycosyltransferase UGT76B1 modulates N-hydroxy-pipecolic acid homeostasis and plant immunity

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: N-hydroxy-pipecolic acid metabolism / salicylic acid glycosylation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: NHP O-glycosylation branch; SA glycosylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream in NHP metabolism; terminal inactivation/storage glycosylation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to N-hydroxy-pipecolic acid to form NHP-OGlc; also glycosylates salicylic acid to SAG and isoleucic acid to ILA-Glc in vitro
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms a modified/inactivated derivative of the target product and controls target-product turnover/homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for NHP turnover/homeostasis; described as central/major role, but not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulator of plant innate immunity and systemic acquired resistance; contributes to defense against biotrophic pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf tissue; local and systemic leaves during SAR-associated responses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Pseudomonas syringae infection; co-expressed with FMO1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae ES4326 infection; UV-C treatment; exogenous D9-NHP infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; CRISPR knockout; targeted and nontargeted metabolomics (UHPLC-HRMS, UPLC-nanoESI-QTRAP-MS); recombinant enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; MS/MS structural validation; genetic interaction with fmo1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for NHP-OGlc accumulation; loss of function abolishes NHP-OGlc and elevates NHP and SA, causing dwarfism and constitutive disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT76B1 was identified as the in vivo and in vitro glycosyltransferase that converts NHP to NHP-OGlc. 2. ugt76b1 mutants completely lacked NHP-OGlc after pathogen infection or UV-C treatment and accumulated strongly elevated NHP. 3. Loss of UGT76B1 caused dwarfism and constitutive resistance, which were suppressed by fmo1 loss of function, showing UGT76B1 acts downstream of FMO1 to maintain NHP homeostasis. 4. Exogenous D9-NHP moved systemically without requiring glycosylation, indicating NHP-OGlc is not required for long-distance movement.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes dwarf phenotype, dark green leaves, constitutive defense/growth penalty
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to tune immunity-growth balance by editing or modulating NHP turnover; strong defense gain may incur dwarfism so partial modulation rather than full knockout is advisable
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UGT76B1 also glycosylates SA and ILA in vitro; authors conclude it is the only enzyme O-glycosylating NHP in planta under their conditions. NHP glycosylation is not required for NHP systemic movement but is crucial for balancing growth and defense.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts pipecolic acid (Pip) to N-hydroxy-pipecolic acid (NHP)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key biosynthetic enzyme for NHP formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulator of plant innate immunity and systemic acquired resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript reduced in ugt76b1 mutant after infection; gain-of-function mutant FMO1-3D shows constitutive resistance phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant; gain-of-function mutant (FMO1-3D); double-mutant genetic suppression with ugt76b1; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for NHP and NHP-OGlc accumulation; loss of FMO1 suppresses elevated resistance and dwarf phenotype of ugt76b1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FMO1 is required for NHP formation because fmo1 mutants accumulated neither NHP nor NHP-OGlc. 2. fmo1 loss suppressed the dwarf and constitutive resistance phenotypes of ugt76b1, demonstrating that UGT76B1 acts downstream of FMO1. 3. FMO1-3D displayed strong resistance consistent with elevated NHP signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Gain-of-function associated with dwarfism/autoimmunity-like growth-defense tradeoff; loss increases susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating FMO1 can strongly alter immunity through NHP production, but excessive activation likely penalizes growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the genetic logic of the study, FMO1 is a pathway-background comparator rather than the primary newly characterized enzyme of the paper.
